questions.question: What does the `font-family` property accept as a fallback mechanism?
choices: Only one font name at a time
A comma-separated list of font names, checked in order
A semicolon-separated list of font names
A single generic font family keyword only
questions.explanation: font-family accepts a comma-separated list of font names. The browser uses the first available font in the list.

questions.question: Which CSS rule is used to load a custom font file for use on a webpage?
questions.explanation: @font-face defines a custom font by specifying its name and the URL to the font file, making it available for use in font-family declarations.

questions.question: What does `text-transform: uppercase` do?
choices: Changes the font weight to bold
Converts all text to uppercase letters
Adds an underline to the text
questions.explanation: text-transform: uppercase converts all characters in the element to uppercase, regardless of how they are typed in the HTML.

questions.question: Which property controls the vertical spacing between lines of text?
questions.explanation: line-height sets the height of each line box, controlling the vertical space between lines of text.

questions.question: The ___ property removes or adds decorations like underlines on text.
questions.explanation: text-decoration controls decorative lines on text, including underline, overline, line-through, and none.

questions.question: Setting `text-align: ___` horizontally centers text within its container.
questions.explanation: text-align: center centers inline content (including text) horizontally within a block-level container.
